extraction.one_line_summary: GREIPEL Andre won the sprint with minimal team support after surviving the final climb, beating DEGENKOLB and GROENEWEGEN in the finale
tactical_patterns: GREIPEL won without organized leadout train in final 5km, self-positioning proves decisive when team infrastructure unavailable
GROENEWEGEN struggled on final climb then faded in sprint, confirming climbing weakness compromises sprint power even on mild gradients
QUICKSTEP's KITTEL received SABATINI leadout but still lost, suggesting their sprint train assembly failed at critical juncture

rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: positive: Won without team support in final kilometers, demonstrating superior tactical self-sufficiency and positioning instincts
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: Struggled on final climb and faded badly after fighting back, showing insufficient recovery capacity for undulating sprint stages
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: neutral: Positioned perfectly behind DEGENKOLB but failed to convert, suggesting positioning skill exceeds finishing punch
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: Led out sprint but immediately swamped, indicating poor timing judgment or insufficient peak speed
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: Yellow jersey focused solely on avoiding splits rather than contesting sprint, revealing conservative GC protection tactics
